scope.name: TOPOLOGY_EVIDENCE_PRECEDENCE
scope.purpose: Define deterministic evidence-precedence semantics for distributed reconciliation so conflicting topology reports resolve through lineage-bound artifacts rather than node voting, raw observation, or inferred legitimacy.
scope.arbitration_role: compare, rank, arbitrate, classify — not repair, rewrite, create authority, or create legitimacy

convergence_requirements.description: Reconciliation convergence requires all three conditions.

decisive_artifact_requirements.description: A conflict may converge only when a decisive legitimacy artifact exists and can be:

evidence_tiers.description: Canonical evidence artifact classes with deterministic precedence order. Verified Tier 1 > Tier 2 > Tier 3 > Tier 4.

tiers.label: TIER_1
tiers.name: Verified Immutable Lineage Anchors
tiers.description: Cryptographically verified, append-only ledger entries with confirmed integrity. Highest precedence only when integrity verifies.

tiers.label: TIER_2
tiers.name: Signed Registry State
tiers.description: Current registry state with verified signatures. Mutable representation that may challenge Tier 1 integrity but may not override verified Tier 1 evidence.

tiers.label: TIER_3
tiers.name: Topology Observations
tiers.description: Runtime topology intelligence observations. Observer-derived, non-authoritative visibility evidence. Never overrides registry state.

tiers.label: TIER_4
tiers.name: Derived Reconstruction
tiers.description: State derived from partial evidence when direct observation is unavailable. Lowest precedence. Never overrides observed evidence.

evidence_tiers.integrity_suspension_boundary: suspected Tier 1 truncation ≠ verified Tier 1 anchor

arbitration_rules.description: Deterministic rules for resolving conflicting evidence across tiers.

rule_a.name: Verified Tier 1 vs Tier 2 Disagreement
rule_a.condition: Tier 1 integrity verifies AND Tier 2 disagrees
rule_a.reason: current registry state is mutable/reporting state; verified lineage anchor is immutable lineage evidence

rule_b.name: Tier 1 Integrity Failure
rule_b.condition: Tier 1 missing OR Tier 1 hash chain broken OR Tier 1 epoch mismatch OR Tier 1 truncation suspected
rule_b.core_rule: Do not fall back to mutable registry truth. Suspend closure.

rule_c.name: Tier 2 Registry Disagreement
rule_c.condition: Tier 2 sources disagree (e.g. replay_registry = CONSUMED, authority_registry = PENDING)

rule_d.name: Tier 3 Observation Conflicts with Tier 2 Registry
rule_d.condition: Tier 3 topology observation conflicts with Tier 2 signed registry state

rule_e.name: Tier 4 Reconstruction Conflicts with Tier 1–3
rule_e.condition: Derived reconstruction conflicts with Tier 1, Tier 2, or Tier 3 evidence
rule_e.core_rule: Derived inference never overrides observed evidence.

conflict_resolution_semantics.description: How conflicting evidence is ranked, classified, and prevented from creating false closure.
conflict_resolution_semantics.resolution_method: deterministic evaluation of lineage-bound legitimacy artifacts

conflicting_high_precedence_artifacts.description: When two verified Tier 1 artifacts conflict (e.g. revocation record vs proof lineage record for same object)

unresolved_state_fallback_semantics.description: Safe outcome when precedence cannot be determined.
unresolved_state_fallback_semantics.core_rule: Arbitration determines precedence. Arbitration does not create truth. When precedence cannot be determined, fail closed.

downgrade_and_suspension_semantics.description: Conditions under which legitimacy is downgraded or suspended without full revocation.
downgrade_and_suspension_semantics.core_rule: Suspension does not create legitimacy. Suspension prevents false closure.

resolution.if_unverified: suspend local certainty, classify conflict, require reconciliation

case_2_proof_missing_vs_proof_present.node_b: reports PROOF_PRESENT with proof lineage record

case_3_state_match_missing_lineage.scenario: Observed reality hash equals validated object hash, proof is missing, lineage is missing

resolution.core_rule: state match is evidence only; state match cannot create legitimacy

arbitration_output_contract.description: Required fields in every arbitration output.

determinism_requirements.same_evidence_hashes: same evidence hashes → same winning evidence hash

non_authoritative_boundary.core_rule: Arbitration determines precedence. Arbitration does not create truth.

#1640.description: Runtime Topology Intelligence Planning. Evidence precedence derives from the evidence_precedence_hierarchy and gap_bridgeability_semantics defined in #1640.

#1641.description: Classified Observation Object Spec. Tier 3 evidence classes are classified_observation_objects as defined in #1641.

#1642.description: Topology Emission Boundary Planning. Emission of classified observations is governed by #1642; this spec consumes emitted observations as Tier 3 evidence.

#1643.description: Legitimacy Conflict State Taxonomy. Conflict states such as LEGITIMACY_UNKNOWN, UNRESOLVED, CONFLICTED, PARTITION_SUSPECTED, and REVOKED_LINEAGE_DETECTED from #1643 inform the classification outputs of this arbitration layer.

#1644.description: This spec. Defines how conflicting evidence is ranked, classified, and prevented from creating false closure.
